v.CNV: 神的灵降在俄德的儿子亚撒利雅身上，
v.ESV: The Spirit of God came upon Azariah the son of Oded,
v.GAE: 하나님의 영이 오뎃의 아들 아사랴에게 임하시매
v.KJV: And the Spirit of God came upon Azariah the son of Oded:
v.NASB: Now the Spirit of God came on Azariah the son of Oded,
v.NIV: The Spirit of God came on Azariah son of Oded.
v.NRSV: The spirit of God came upon Azariah son of Oded.
v.SAENEW: 하나님의 영이 오뎃의 아들 아사랴에게 내리니,
v.WLB: 하나님의 영이 오뎃의 아들 아사랴에게 임했습니다.

v.CNV: 亚撒利雅就出来迎接亚撒，对他说：“亚撒和犹大、便雅悯所有的人哪！请听我说。你们跟随耶和华，耶和华就和你们同在；你们寻求他，就必给你们寻见；你们离弃他，他必离弃你们。
v.ESV: and he went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: The Lord is with you while you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.
v.GAE: 그가 나가서 아사를 맞아 이르되 아사와 및 유다와 베냐민의 무리들아 내 말을 들으라 너희가 여호와와 함께 하면 여호와께서 너희와 함께 하실지라 너희가 만일 그를 찾으면 그가 너희와 만나게 되시려니와 너희가 만일 그를 버리면 그도 너희를 버리시리라
v.KJV: And he went out to meet Asa, and said unto him, Hear ye me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin; The LORD is with you, while ye be with him; and if ye seek him, he will be found of you; but if ye forsake him, he will forsake you.
v.NASB: and he went out to meet Asa and said to him, 'Listen to me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: the LORD is with you when you are with Him. And if you seek Him, He will let you find Him; but if you forsake Him, He will forsake you.
v.NIV: He went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Listen to me, Asa and all Judah and Benjamin. The Lord is with you when you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.
v.NRSV: He went out to meet Asa and said to him, “Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin: The LORD is with you, while you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you abandon him, he will abandon you.
v.SAENEW: 그가 아사 앞에 나아가서 다음과 같이 말하였다. "아사 임금님과 온 유다와 베냐민은, 제가 하는 말을 들으십시오. 임금님과 백성이 주님을 떠나지 않는 한, 주님께서도 임금님과 백성을 떠나지 않으실 것입니다. 임금님과 백성이 그를 찾으면, 그가 만나 주실 것입니다. 그러나 임금님과 백성이 그를 버리면, 주님께서도 임금님과 백성을 버리실 것입니다.
v.WLB: 그는 아사를 만나러 나가 말했습니다. “아사 왕과 온 유다와 베냐민 지파야, 내 말을 잘 들으라. 너희가 여호와와 함께하는 한 여호와께서 너희와 함께 계실 것이다. 너희가 그분을 찾으면 그분을 찾게 될 것이지만 너희가 그분을 버리면 그분이 너희를 버리실 것이다.

v.CNV: 以色列人没有真神，没有教导他们的祭司，没有律法，已经很久了。
v.ESV: For a long time Israel was without the true God, and without a teaching priest and without law,
v.GAE: 이스라엘에는 참 신이 없고 가르치는 제사장도 없고 율법도 없은 지가 오래 되었으나
v.KJV: Now for a long season Israel hath been without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law.
v.NASB: 'For many days Israel was without the true God and without a teaching priest and without law.
v.NIV: For a long time Israel was without the true God, without a priest to teach and without the law.
v.NRSV: For a long time Israel was without the true God, and without a teaching priest, and without law;
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘은 오랫동안 참 하나님이 없이 지내 왔습니다. 가르치는 제사장도 없었고 율법도 없었습니다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘은 오랫동안 참 하나님이 없었고 가르치는 제사장도 율법도 없이 지내왔다.

v.CNV: 但他们在遭难的时候，回转归向耶和华以色列的　神，寻求他，他就给他们寻见。
v.ESV: but when in their distress they turned to the Lord , the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.
v.GAE: 그들이 그 환난 때에 이스라엘 하나님 여호와께로 돌아가서 찾으매 그가 그들과 만나게 되셨나니
v.KJV: But when they in their trouble did turn unto the LORD God of Israel, and sought him, he was found of them.
v.NASB: 'But in their distress they turned to the LORD God of Israel, and they sought Him, and He let them find Him.
v.NIV: But in their distress they turned to the Lord , the God of Israel, and sought him, and he was found by them.
v.NRSV: but when in their distress they turned to the LORD, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 이스라엘이 어려운 일을 만나서, 주 이스라엘의 하나님께 돌아와 그를 찾으면, 주님께서는 그들을 만나 주셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그 어려움 가운데서 이스라엘의 하나님 여호와께 돌아와 그분을 찾으므로 하나님께서는 백성들을 만나 주셨다.

v.CNV: 那时，出入的人都没有平安，因为各国的居民都遭遇动乱。
v.ESV: In those times there was no peace to him who went out or to him who came in, for great disturbances afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
v.GAE: 그 때에 온 땅의 모든 주민이 크게 요란하여 사람의 출입이 평안하지 못하며
v.KJV: And in those times there was no peace to him that went out, nor to him that came in, but great vexations were upon all the inhabitants of the countries.
v.NASB: 'In those times there was no peace to him who went out or to him who came in, for many disturbances afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
v.NIV: In those days it was not safe to travel about, for all the inhabitants of the lands were in great turmoil.
v.NRSV: In those times it was not safe for anyone to go or come, for great disturbances afflicted all the inhabitants of the lands.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에는 세상이 하도 어지러워서, 땅 위에 사는 모든 백성이 마음 놓고 평안히 나들이도 못하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그때 나가는 사람이나 들어오는 사람에게 평안이 없었다. 그 땅에 사는 모든 사람들이 큰 혼란에 빠져 있었다.

v.CNV: 这国攻击那国，这城攻击那城，因为　神用各样灾难使他们纷乱。
v.ESV: They were broken in pieces. Nation was crushed by nation and city by city, for God troubled them with every sort of distress.
v.GAE: 이 나라와 저 나라가 서로 치고 이 성읍이 저 성읍과 또한 그러하여 피차 상한 바 되었나니 이는 하나님이 여러 가지 고난으로 요란하게 하셨음이라
v.KJV: And nation was destroyed of nation, and city of city: for God did vex them with all adversity.
v.NASB: 'Nation was crushed by nation, and city by city, for God troubled them with every kind of distress.
v.NIV: One nation was being crushed by another and one city by another, because God was troubling them with every kind of distress.
v.NRSV: They were broken in pieces, nation against nation and city against city, for God troubled them with every sort of distress.
v.SAENEW: 나라와 나라가, 성읍과 성읍이 서로 치고 무찌르는 판이었습니다. 이것은 하나님께서 사람들이 온갖 고난 속에서 고통을 받도록 버려 두셨기 때문이었습니다.
v.WLB: 민족이 민족을 치고 성읍이 성읍을 치니 하나님께서 모든 고난으로 그들을 힘들게 하셨다.

v.CNV: 但你们要坚强，不要胆怯，因为你们所行的，必有赏报。”
v.ESV: But you, take courage! Do not let your hands be weak, for your work shall be rewarded.”
v.GAE: 그런즉 너희는 강하게 하라 너희의 손이 약하지 않게 하라 너희 행위에는 상급이 있음이라 하니라
v.KJV: Be ye strong therefore, and let not your hands be weak: for your work shall be rewarded.
v.NASB: 'But you, be strong and do not lose courage, for there is reward for your work.'
v.NIV: But as for you, be strong and do not give up, for your work will be rewarded.”
v.NRSV: But you, take courage! Do not let your hands be weak, for your work shall be rewarded.”
v.SAENEW: 그러나 임금님과 백성은 기운을 내십시오. 낙심하지 마십시오. 임금님과 백성이 하는 수고에는 상급이 따를 것입니다."
v.WLB: 그러나 너희는 강해지고 포기하지 말라. 너희가 한 일에 대해 상이 있을 것이다.”

v.CNV: 亚撒听了这些话，和俄德的儿子亚撒利雅先知所说的预言以后，就勇敢起来，在犹大、便雅悯全地，以及以法莲山地夺取的各城，把可憎的偶像除去，又在耶和华殿的走廊前面，重新修造耶和华的祭坛。
v.ESV: As soon as Asa heard these words, the prophecy of Azariah the son of Oded, he took courage and put away the detestable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities that he had taken in the hill country of Ephraim, and he repaired the altar of the Lord that was in front of the vestibule of the house of the Lord .
v.GAE: 아사가 이 말 곧 선지자 오뎃의 예언을 듣고 마음을 강하게 하여 가증한 물건들을 유다와 베냐민 온 땅에서 없애고 또 에브라임 산지에서 빼앗은 성읍들에서도 없애고 또 여호와의 낭실 앞에 있는 여호와의 제단을 재건하고
v.KJV: And when Asa heard these words, and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he took courage, and put away the abominable idols out of all the land of Judah and Benjamin, and out of the cities which he had taken from mount Ephraim, and renewed the altar of the LORD, that was before the porch of the LORD.
v.NASB: Now when Asa heard these words and the prophecy which Azariah the son of Oded the prophet spoke, he took courage and removed the abominable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the cities which he had captured in the hill country of Ephraim. He then restored the altar of the LORD which was in front of the porch of the LORD.
v.NIV: When Asa heard these words and the prophecy of Azariah son of Oded the prophet, he took courage. He removed the detestable idols from the whole land of Judah and Benjamin and from the towns he had captured in the hills of Ephraim. He repaired the altar of the Lord that was in front of the portico of the Lord ’s temple.
v.NRSV: When Asa heard these words, the prophecy of Azariah son of Oded, he took courage, and put away the abominable idols from all the land of Judah and Benjamin and from the towns that he had taken in the hill country of Ephraim. He repaired the altar of the LORD that was in front of the vestibule of the house of the LORD.
v.SAENEW: 아사는 이 모든 말, 곧 오뎃의 아들 아사랴 예언자가 전하여 주는 예언을 듣고, 용기를 내어, 유다와 베냐민 온 지방과 에브라임 산간지역의 점령지역 성읍에서 역겨운 물건들을 없애 버렸다. 그는 또 주님의 성전 현관 앞에 있는 주님의 제단을 보수하였다.
v.WLB: 아사는 이 말, 곧 오뎃의 아들인 예언자 아사랴의 예언을 듣고 용기를 얻었습니다. 아사 왕은 유다와 베냐민 온 땅 그리고 에브라임 산지에서 빼앗은 성들에서 그 역겨운 우상들을 없애 버렸습니다. 그는 또 여호와의 성전 현관 앞에 있는 여호와의 제단을 고쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 他又召聚了犹大和便雅悯所有的人，以及在他们中间寄居的以法莲人、玛拿西人、西缅人。以色列人看见了耶和华亚撒的　神和他同在，就有很多人投奔亚撒。
v.ESV: And he gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and those from Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon who were residing with them, for great numbers had deserted to him from Israel when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.
v.GAE: 또 유다와 베냐민의 무리를 모으고 에브라임과 므낫세와 시므온 가운데에서 나와서 저희 중에 머물러 사는 자들을 모았으니 이는 이스라엘 사람들이 아사의 하나님 여호와께서 그와 함께 하심을 보고 아사에게로 돌아오는 자가 많았음이더라
v.KJV: And he gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and the strangers with them out of Ephraim and Manasseh, and out of Simeon: for they fell to him out of Israel in abundance, when they saw that the LORD his God was with him.
v.NASB: He gathered all Judah and Benjamin and those from Ephraim, Manasseh and Simeon who resided with them, for many defected to him from Israel when they saw that the LORD his God was with him.
v.NIV: Then he assembled all Judah and Benjamin and the people from Ephraim, Manasseh and Simeon who had settled among them, for large numbers had come over to him from Israel when they saw that the Lord his God was with him.
v.NRSV: He gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and those from Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon who were residing as aliens with them, for great numbers had deserted to him from Israel when they saw that the LORD his God was with him.
v.SAENEW: 그는 또 유다와 베냐민의 모든 백성을 불러모으고, 그들에게로 와서 함께 살고 있는, 에브라임과 므낫세와 시므온 지파 소속의 백성도 모두 불러모았다. 주 하나님께서 아사와 함께 계시는 것을 보고, 이스라엘에서도 많은 사람들이 아사에게로 모여들었다.
v.WLB: 그는 온 유다와 베냐민 사람들과 그들 가운데 살고 있던 에브라임, 므낫세, 시므온 사람들을 불러 모았습니다. 하나님 여호와께서 아사와 함께하시는 것을 보고 이스라엘에서도 많은 사람들이 아사에게로 돌아왔습니다.

v.CNV: 亚撒在位第十五年三月，他们聚集在耶路撒冷。
v.ESV: They were gathered at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
v.GAE: 아사 왕 제십오년 셋째 달에 그들이 예루살렘에 모이고
v.KJV: So they gathered themselves together at Jerusalem in the third month, in the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
v.NASB: So they assembled at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of Asa's reign.
v.NIV: They assembled at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of Asa’s reign.
v.NRSV: They were gathered at Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of the reign of Asa.
v.SAENEW: 그들이 예루살렘에 모인 것은 아사 왕 십오년이 되던 해 세 번째 달이었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 아사가 다스린 지 15년 셋째 달에 예루살렘에 모였습니다.

v.CNV: 那一天，他们从夺回来的战利品当中，把七百头牛，七千只羊，献给耶和华为祭，
v.ESV: They sacrificed to the Lord on that day from the spoil that they had brought 700 oxen and 7,000 sheep.
v.GAE: 그 날에 노략하여 온 물건 중에서 소 칠백 마리와 양 칠천 마리로 여호와께 제사를 지내고
v.KJV: And they offered unto the LORD the same time, of the spoil which they had brought, seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep.
v.NASB: They sacrificed to the LORD that day 700 oxen and 7,000 sheep from the spoil they had brought.
v.NIV: At that time they sacrificed to the Lord seven hundred head of cattle and seven thousand sheep and goats from the plunder they had brought back.
v.NRSV: They sacrificed to the LORD on that day, from the booty that they had brought, seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep.
v.SAENEW: 그 날 그들은 그들이 가져 온 전리품 가운데서 소 칠백 마리와 양 칠천 마리를 주님께 희생제물로 잡아 바치며,
v.WLB: 그날 그들은 자기들이 빼앗은 것 가운데 소 700마리, 양 7,000마리를 여호와께 제물로 올려 드렸습니다.

v.CNV: 他们也立约，要一心一意寻求耶和华他们列祖的　神。
v.ESV: And they entered into a covenant to seek the Lord , the God of their fathers, with all their heart and with all their soul,
v.GAE: 또 마음을 다하고 목숨을 다하여 조상들의 하나님 여호와를 찾기로 언약하고
v.KJV: And they entered into a covenant to seek the LORD God of their fathers with all their heart and with all their soul;
v.NASB: They entered into the covenant to seek the LORD God of their fathers with all their heart and soul;
v.NIV: They entered into a covenant to seek the Lord , the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and soul.
v.NRSV: They entered into a covenant to seek the LORD, the God of their ancestors, with all their heart and with all their soul.
v.SAENEW: 마음을 다하고 정성을 다하여 주 조상의 하나님만을 찾기로 하는 언약을 맺었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 온 마음과 영혼을 다해 조상들의 하나님 여호와를 찾겠다고 약속했습니다.

v.CNV: 不寻求耶和华以色列的　神的，无论男女老幼，都必处死。
v.ESV: but that whoever would not seek the Lord , the God of Israel, should be put to death, whether young or old, man or woman.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 하나님 여호와를 찾지 아니하는 자는 대소 남녀를 막론하고 죽이는 것이 마땅하다 하고
v.KJV: That whosoever would not seek the LORD God of Israel should be put to death, whether small or great, whether man or woman.
v.NASB: and whoever would not seek the LORD God of Israel should be put to death, whether small or great, man or woman.
v.NIV: All who would not seek the Lord , the God of Israel, were to be put to death, whether small or great, man or woman.
v.NRSV: Whoever would not seek the LORD, the God of Israel, should be put to death, whether young or old, man or woman.
v.SAENEW: 주 이스라엘의 하나님을 찾지 아니하는 자는, 젊은 사람이든지 나이 많은 사람이든지, 남자든지 여자든지 가릴 것 없이, 누구든지 다 죽이기로 하였다.
v.WLB: 이스라엘의 하나님 여호와를 찾지 않는 사람들은 작든 크든, 남녀를 불문하고 다 죽이기로 했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们就大声欢呼，吹起号角，向耶和华起誓。
v.ESV: They swore an oath to the Lord with a loud voice and with shouting and with trumpets and with horns.
v.GAE: 무리가 큰 소리로 외치며 피리와 나팔을 불어 여호와께 맹세하매
v.KJV: And they sware unto the LORD with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with cornets.
v.NASB: Moreover, they made an oath to the LORD with a loud voice, with shouting, with trumpets and with horns.
v.NIV: They took an oath to the Lord with loud acclamation, with shouting and with trumpets and horns.
v.NRSV: They took an oath to the LORD with a loud voice, and with shouting, and with trumpets, and with horns.
v.SAENEW: 사람들은 함성과 쇠나팔 소리와 뿔나팔 소리가 울려 퍼지는 가운데, 주님께 큰소리로 맹세하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 큰 소리로 부르고 나팔 소리와 뿔 소리로 여호와께 맹세했습니다.

v.CNV: 犹大众人都为了所起的誓快乐，因为他们一心起誓，尽意寻求耶和华，耶和华就给他们寻见，并且使他们四境安宁。
v.ESV: And all Judah rejoiced over the oath, for they had sworn with all their heart and had sought him with their whole desire, and he was found by them, and the Lord gave them rest all around.
v.GAE: 온 유다가 이 맹세를 기뻐한지라 무리가 마음을 다하여 맹세하고 뜻을 다하여 여호와를 찾았으므로 여호와께서도 그들을 만나 주시고 그들의 사방에 평안을 주셨더라
v.KJV: And all Judah rejoiced at the oath: for they had sworn with all their heart, and sought him with their whole desire; and he was found of them: and the LORD gave them rest round about.
v.NASB: All Judah rejoiced concerning the oath, for they had sworn with their whole heart and had sought Him earnestly, and He let them find Him. So the LORD gave them rest on every side.
v.NIV: All Judah rejoiced about the oath because they had sworn it wholeheartedly. They sought God eagerly, and he was found by them. So the Lord gave them rest on every side.
v.NRSV: All Judah rejoiced over the oath; for they had sworn with all their heart, and had sought him with their whole desire, and he was found by them, and the LORD gave them rest all around.
v.SAENEW: 온 유다 백성은 이러한 맹세를 하는 것이 기쁘기만 하였다. 그들은 마음을 다해 맹세하고, 정성을 다해 주님을 찾았으므로, 주님께서 그들을 만나 주셨고, 사방으로 그들에게 평안을 주셨다.
v.WLB: 온 유다는 그들이 마음을 다해 맹세한 것이 기뻤습니다. 그들은 마음을 다해 하나님을 찾았고 여호와께서도 백성들과 만나 주셨습니다. 여호와께서는 그들 주변에 평안을 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 亚撒王也废掉他的祖母玛迦太后的位，因为她曾给亚舍拉做了可憎的像；亚撒把她那可憎的像砍了下来，捣得粉碎，烧在汲沦溪旁。
v.ESV: Even Maacah, his mother, King Asa removed from being queen mother because she had made a detestable image for Asherah. Asa cut down her image, crushed it, and burned it at the brook Kidron.
v.GAE: 아사 왕의 어머니 마아가가 아세라의 가증한 목상을 만들었으므로 아사가 그의 태후의 자리를 폐하고 그의 우상을 찍고 빻아 기드론 시냇가에서 불살랐으니
v.KJV: And also concerning Maachah the mother of Asa the king, he removed her from being queen, because she had made an idol in a grove: and Asa cut down her idol, and stamped it, and burnt it at the brook Kidron.
v.NASB: He also removed Maacah, the mother of King Asa, from the position of queen mother, because she had made a horrid image as an Asherah, and Asa cut down her horrid image, crushed it and burned it at the brook Kidron.
v.NIV: King Asa also deposed his grandmother Maakah from her position as queen mother, because she had made a repulsive image for the worship of Asherah. Asa cut it down, broke it up and burned it in the Kidron Valley.
v.NRSV: King Asa even removed his mother Maacah from being queen mother because she had made an abominable image for Asherah. Asa cut down her image, crushed it, and burned it at the Wadi Kidron.
v.SAENEW: 아사 왕은 자기의 할머니 마아가가 혐오스러운 아세라 목상을 만들었다고 해서, 태후의 자리에서 물러나게 하였다. 아사는 자기의 할머니가 만든 혐오스러운 우상을 토막내어 가루로 만들어서, 기드론 냇가에서 불살라 버렸다.
v.WLB: 아사 왕은 또한 자기 어머니 마아가를 대비 자리에서 물러나게 했습니다. 그가 역겨운 아세라 우상을 만들었기 때문입니다. 아사는 그 우상을 찍어 기드론 골짜기에서 태웠습니다.

v.CNV: 只是邱坛还没有从以色列中除掉，但亚撒的心一生都完全忠于耶和华。
v.ESV: But the high places were not taken out of Israel. Nevertheless, the heart of Asa was wholly true all his days.
v.GAE: 산당은 이스라엘 중에서 제하지 아니하였으나 아사의 마음이 일평생 온전하였더라
v.KJV: But the high places were not taken away out of Israel: nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.
v.NASB: But the high places were not removed from Israel; nevertheless Asa's heart was blameless all his days.
v.NIV: Although he did not remove the high places from Israel, Asa’s heart was fully committed to the Lord all his life.
v.NRSV: But the high places were not taken out of Israel. Nevertheless the heart of Asa was true all his days.
v.SAENEW: 그렇다고 산당이 모두 제거된 것은 아니었지만, 주님을 사모하는 아사의 마음은 평생 한결같았다.
v.WLB: 그가 이스라엘에서 산당을 없애지는 않았지만 아사는 그 마음을 평생 여호와께 온전히 바쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 亚撒把他父亲所分别为圣，和他自己所分别为圣的金银和器皿，都送进　神的殿里。
v.ESV: And he brought into the house of God the sacred gifts of his father and his own sacred gifts, silver, and gold, and vessels.
v.GAE: 그가 또 그의 아버지가 구별한 물건과 자기가 구별한 물건 곧 은과 금과 그릇들을 하나님의 전에 드렸더니
v.KJV: And he brought into the house of God the things that his father had dedicated, and that he himself had dedicated, silver, and gold, and vessels.
v.NASB: He brought into the house of God the dedicated things of his father and his own dedicated things: silver and gold and utensils.
v.NIV: He brought into the temple of God the silver and gold and the articles that he and his father had dedicated.
v.NRSV: He brought into the house of God the votive gifts of his father and his own votive gifts — silver, gold, and utensils.
v.SAENEW: 아사는 자기의 아버지와 자신이 거룩하게 구별하여 바친, 은과 금과 그릇들을 하나님의 성전에 들여놓았다.
v.WLB: 아사 왕은 그와 그 아버지가 바친 은과 금과 물건들을 거룩한 예물로 여호와의 성전에 드렸습니다.

v.CNV: 从那时，直到亚撒在位第三十五年，都没有战争。
v.ESV: And there was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
v.GAE: 이 때부터 아사 왕 제삼십오년까지 다시는 전쟁이 없으니라
v.KJV: And there was no more war unto the five and thirtieth year of the reign of Asa.
v.NASB: And there was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa's reign.
v.NIV: There was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa’s reign.
v.NRSV: And there was no more war until the thirty-fifth year of the reign of Asa.
v.SAENEW: 이 때부터 아사 왕 삼십오년까지 전쟁이 없었다.
v.WLB: 아사가 다스린 지 35년째까지 더 전쟁이 없었습니다.
